briefs.title: Council to Consider: Port Coquitlam Releases Major 2027 Infrastructure Project Approvals
briefs.summary: Port Coquitlam's Committee of Council is set to meet on April 21, 2026, to adopt its agenda and release previously closed-session decisions to the public. Those released decisions include approval of several large 2027 capital projects — meaning road, drainage, and sewer upgrades totalling over $25 million — covering areas like Hyde Creek, Prairie Avenue, Shaughnessy Street, and Western Drive.
briefs.action_required: Check if any approved road or drainage projects affect your business location and plan for possible disruptions.

key_dates.label: Port Coquitlam Committee of Council meeting — April 21, 2026

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Transportation & Logistics, Landlord & Property Mgmt

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Port Coquitlam Adopts April 21, 2026 Meeting Agenda
briefs.summary: Port Coquitlam's Committee of Council is set to vote on formally adopting the agenda for its April 21, 2026 meeting. This is a routine procedural step — it simply confirms the list of topics the committee will discuss that day. No business decisions are made at this stage.

briefs.title: Council to confirm April 7 Committee minutes covering utility billing, capital reserves, and election appointments
briefs.summary: Port Coquitlam Council is set to vote on confirming the minutes from its April 7, 2026 Committee of Council meeting. Those minutes cover three notable items: a referral to staff to review how utility bills are handled for new developments, a proposal to move roughly $6M from a long-term reserve into capital project funds for 2027, and the appointment of election officers for the 2026 municipal election.
briefs.action_required: If you own or develop property in Port Coquitlam, watch for the utility billing review — it could affect your costs.

briefs.title: Council to Consider Reports: No Details Available Yet
briefs.summary: The Port Coquitlam Council agenda lists a 'Reports' item for April 21, 2026, but no content or details have been provided. There is not enough information available to summarize what Council will be considering or voting on at this time.
briefs.action_required: Check the Port Coquitlam Council website for the full agenda and report details before April 21, 2026.

key_dates.label: Port Coquitlam Council meeting — Reports item on agenda

briefs.title: Port Coquitlam Council to Release Details on $33M+ in 2027 Infrastructure Projects
briefs.summary: Port Coquitlam's Committee of Council is set to publicly release decisions made in earlier closed meetings, revealing that council has already approved over $33 million in 2027 capital projects — including flood control work at Hyde Creek ($8.8M), road rebuilding on Prairie Avenue ($9.7M), a new traffic signal at Raleigh Street and Lougheed Highway ($4.3M), and several other drainage and road projects. These were approved in closed session (meaning the public was not present) and are now being made public at the April 21, 2026 meeting.
briefs.action_required: Check if any approved road or drainage projects affect access to your business location and plan ahead.

key_dates.label: Port Coquitlam Committee of Council meeting — infrastructure decisions released to public

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Transportation & Logistics, Landlord & Property Mgmt

briefs.title: Port Coquitlam Council to release previously closed votes on major 2027 infrastructure projects
briefs.summary: Port Coquitlam's Committee of Council is set to meet on April 21, 2026, and will release several decisions made in earlier closed (private) meetings. Those decisions — now being made public — approved over $33 million in road, drainage, and flood-mitigation projects planned for 2027, including work on Hyde Creek, Prairie Avenue, Shaughnessy Street, and several other local roads.
briefs.action_required: Watch for how these road and infrastructure projects may affect access to your business location in 2027.

key_dates.label: Port Coquitlam Committee of Council meeting — closed votes released to public

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Transportation & Logistics, Landlord & Property Mgmt

briefs.title: Council to Release: Port Coquitlam Approves $33M+ in 2027 Infrastructure Projects
briefs.summary: Port Coquitlam's Committee of Council is set to publicly release a series of decisions made in closed meetings earlier this year. These previously private votes approved over $33 million in 2027 capital projects — including flood control work at Hyde Creek ($8.8M), road reconstruction on Prairie Avenue ($9.675M), a new traffic signal at Raleigh Street and Lougheed Highway ($4.31M), drainage improvements on Dominion Avenue ($82,500), and road and sewer upgrades on Shaughnessy Street and Western Drive.
briefs.action_required: If your business is near Hyde Creek, Prairie Ave, Raleigh St, or Shaughnessy St, watch for construction notices starting in 2027.

key_dates.label: Port Coquitlam Committee of Council meeting — agenda items considered

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Transportation & Logistics, Landlord & Property Mgmt

briefs.title: Port Coquitlam Council to Release Previously Closed Votes on Six Major Infrastructure Projects
briefs.summary: Port Coquitlam Council is set to make public several infrastructure decisions that were previously voted on behind closed doors. The projects — totalling over $33 million — include flood control work at Hyde Creek, road rebuilds on Prairie Avenue and Shaughnessy Street, a new traffic signal at Raleigh and Lougheed, drainage fixes on Dominion Avenue, and upgrades near Cedar Creek and Western Drive, all planned for 2027.
briefs.action_required: Watch for construction timelines on these projects — road closures and detours could affect your business access in 2027.

briefs.title: Council to Consider Closing April 21, 2026 Meeting to Discuss Land and Services
briefs.summary: Port Coquitlam's Committee of Council is set to vote on closing part of its April 21, 2026 meeting to the public. The closed session would cover topics like land deals and early-stage talks about new city services — items that could harm the city's interests if discussed openly. Separately, the agenda also releases details of several major infrastructure projects already approved in earlier closed meetings, totalling over $33 million.
briefs.action_required: Watch the April 21, 2026 Port Coquitlam Council meeting for decisions that may affect local infrastructure or city services.

key_dates.label: Port Coquitlam Committee of Council meeting — closed session vote

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Transportation & Logistics, Professional Services
